title: Card payments for buskers and street musicians — 0% cut · live.tips
meta_description: Busking without cash? Take card payments from one QR code on the case. Fans scan, pick an amount, leave a message — and every tip lands directly in your own Stripe account. 0% cut, open source.
twitter_description: Card payments for buskers — one QR code on the case, and every tip lands directly in your own Stripe account. 0% cut, open source.
og_image_alt: live.tips — card payments for buskers, on stage in minutes
jsonld_description: Open-source live tip jar for buskers, street musicians and bar bands: take card payments from one QR code on stage, and every tip lands directly in the artist's own Stripe account — 0% cut, no middleman.
hero_badge: Open source · 0% cut · powered by your own Stripe
hero_h1:
Card payments for buskers.
On stage in minutes.
hero_sub: One QR code on the guitar case. Fans scan, pick an amount, leave a name and a message — and every card tip lands directly in your own Stripe account. No platform, no middleman, no cut.
hero_trust: No account needed, no install — with no account your Stripe key never leaves your device; sign in and it moves to our server for your other devices
stage_caption: This is the real renderer, live in your browser — the audience-facing stage every fan sees during a set. Every coin is a real tip pouring in.
how_h2: From soundcheck to your first card tip in three steps
step1_p: Create a restricted API key in your own Stripe dashboard — two minutes, fully guided — and paste it in. The app creates your pay-what-you-want tip link automatically.
step2_p: Print the poster, tape it to the guitar case, put it on the merch table. Fans scan, pick an amount, leave a name and a message — Stripe handles the payment.
step3_p: Your tablet on stage shows every tip in real time against tonight's goal — 3D jar, messages, confetti. Edit the goal mid-set; lock the screen while it sits out front.
alt_pay_p: Optional, alongside Stripe: switch them on and the same QR still opens one tip page listing every method you accept — no separate code per app. Revolut, MobilePay and Monzo tips appear on your stage screen instantly while the fan pays in their app — shown as unverified, since those apps can't confirm the payment.
req_h2: Let the crowd pick the setlist — and tip for it
req_lede: Switch on song requests and your tip jar becomes a live request queue. Fans pick a song from your list and tip to push it up — every request is a real tip landing in your own account, and you still decide what actually gets played.
req1_p: Fans request from the list you choose. The more a song is tipped, the higher it climbs — a live leaderboard the whole room can see.
req2_p: There's no separate “vote” currency. A request is a tip like any other, in the money you already take — it just carries a song with it.
req3_p: The queue is a suggestion, not an order. Play what you want, when you want — the requests just show you where the room's money and mood are.
req_card_note: Fans tip to bump a song up the queue
scale_kicker: From a guitar case to a house tablet
scale_h2: Built for one busker or a whole venue
scale_lede: The same app scales from a phone on the windowsill to a tablet that lives on the bar all night.
scale1_p: Leave a tablet out front and lock it into venue mode: it shows the jar and takes tips and requests, but never exposes your settings, your history or your Stripe key. Made for a screen strangers will touch.
scale2_p: Solo tonight, the full band on Friday. Keep a separate profile for each — its own QR, goal and tip history — and switch between them in a tap.
scale3_p: Share a house tablet between acts. Everyone signs into their own account, sees only their own money, and signs out clean — nothing leaks between them.
sec_lede: With no account there is no live.tips server at all: the app talks straight to api.stripe.com and nothing else, and your Stripe key never leaves your device. Sign in and that key moves to our server — encrypted so even we can't read it — and Stripe reports your tips to us so your other devices see them. Either way the money goes straight from your fan to your own account; we never touch it. Optional Revolut, MobilePay and Monzo tips go through a tiny open-source relay that sees no money.
sec1_h3: Bring your own key, keep your own key
sec1_p: The restricted key is stored in your device's keychain and sent only to Stripe, over TLS. Sign in for a second device and it also syncs, in a private document only your account can read. Full live secret keys are refused outright.
sec2_p: The key can create your tip link and watch tips arrive — nothing else. It cannot touch balances, payouts, refunds, or customer data.
sec3_h3: Refunds and payouts stay in Stripe
sec3_p: Money matters stay where they belong: in your own Stripe dashboard, under your own account.
perm_note: Everything else stays “None” — no payouts, no refunds, no balance access.
mini1_p: A progress bar the whole room can root for. Edit the goal mid-set without stopping the session.
mini2_p: Blocks casual tampering while the device sits out front. Unlock with Face ID or your PIN.
mini3_p: QR posters in several themes and languages, ready for the merch table.
mini4_p: Try a full simulated session before connecting Stripe — no account needed.
cta_p: Runs in your browser today — no install, no signup. Native apps and more payment methods are on the roadmap.
cta_foot: Made for buskers, bar bands and street artists — by one of you.
foot_note: Open source under the MIT license. Tips go straight to the artist's own Stripe account — live.tips never touches the money.
banner: This site is also available in English.
